brief_summary: Four-row horizontal comparison ladder showing tactile bump profiles for Gateron Lanes, Durock T1, Gazzew Boba U4T, and Drop Holy Panda X. Each row displays a force-curve glyph sketched along a travel baseline (left = start of press, right = bottom-out). Lanes: a coral-highlighted sharp spike near the left of travel with immediate decay. T1: a slightly broader spike with a moderate descent. U4T: a D-shaped flat-topped plateau. HPX: a smooth rounded arc that blends into the bottom-out. Right-margin character labels name each switch's bump category. The visual argument positions the Lanes as the only switch in the set chasing the Alps front-loaded snap character.
data_sources: gateron-lanes-tactile-deep-dive.mdx § 'Sound and feel profile' — 'The Lanes is sharper and lighter-feeling ... delivers less of the "hammering a bump" sensation that U4T partisans are after'
gateron-lanes-tactile-deep-dive.mdx § 'Sound and feel profile' — 'The Durock T1 sits between those two profiles — a sharp front edge to the bump, less plateau than the U4T'

gateron-lanes-tactile-deep-dive.mdx § 'Sound and feel profile' — 'The Boba U4T delivers a D-shaped bump — pronounced, plateau-like, designed to be felt unambiguously on every keystroke at every typing speed'

gateron-lanes-tactile-deep-dive.mdx § 'Sound and feel profile' — 'the bump arrives and is over before the bottom-out is a question. There is no ambiguous zone between the tactile event and the landing.'

drop-holy-panda-x-deep-dive.mdx § 'The rounded bump, geometrically' — 'The Holy Panda lineage delivers neither. The rounded bump on a Halo-derived stem is a smooth, symmetric arc ... a fall-off that blends into the bottom-out rather than dropping off cleanly first.'
